summary: Stancil argues that the Looker–Tableau integration signals a structural split in BI: governance/modeling (LookML) is separating from visualization and exploration. This shift redefines BI away from all-in-one suites toward a metrics/governance layer paired with best-in-class exploration tools, with big implications for vendor consolidation and the economics of the modern data stack.
money_quotes: Looker’s crown jewel is LookML; Tableau’s is visualization.
Major BI tools are now also recognizing the combination of governance and consumption can’t hold.
In other words, BI as we know it is dead. BI as global exploration is ascendent.
The list of what we need in a modern data stack... is getting long.
By choosing Tableau, Google’s conceding, at least for the time being, that their data stack ends with LookML’s metrics layer.
key_insight: The Looker–Tableau integration marks a decisive unbundling of BI, with governance/metrics becoming a separate layer and exploration tools defining the new BI landscape.
